title: Responsive Design with HTML Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags for creating responsive HTML web pages.

questions.question: Which meta tag tells the browser to match the page width to the device's width for mobile responsiveness?
options: <meta charset='UTF-8'>
<meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0'>
<meta name='responsive' content='true'>
<meta name='mobile-optimized' content='yes'>

questions.question: Which HTML5 element is preferred for serving different image versions based on screen size (media queries)?
options: <img>
<source>
<picture>
<figure>

questions.question: The 'srcset' attribute on an <img> tag allows for what?
options: Aligning the image on the left or right of the screen.
Providing multiple image file options based on screen density.
Connecting the image to an external CSS stylesheet.
Creating a transparent background for the image.

questions.question: According to responsive web design principles, should you use fixed-width containers in your HTML?
options: Yes, 1024px is the industry standard.
No, it's better to use percentage-based or fluid widths.
Only for mobile devices.
Only if you have a lot of content.

questions.question: What is the result of missing the 'viewport' meta tag on a mobile device?
options: The browser will not load the page at all.
The page will appear as if it's on a desktop screen and text will be very small.
The colors of the page will be distorted.
The browser will automatically add the tag for the user.
